title: Sarvam Samvaad migration plan (research + decision doc)
description: Produce a decision-ready migration plan for moving the voice-calling agent from the custom VoiceLink+Sarvam stack to Sarvam Samvaad (Sarvam's managed conversational-voice platform, claims sub-500ms latency). Driver: sarvam-30b reasoning delay (~5-10s) is unacceptable on live calls and not disableable via public API. Deliverable: docs/samvaad-migration-plan.md. Must cover: (1) what Samvaad is + how to get access (user has dashboard.sarvam.ai; access may be gated behind a waitlist/sales form); (2) telephony integration options (keep VoiceLink DID + bridge WS audio into Samvaad, VoiceLink DID -> SIP forward, or switch to a Samvaad partner telco like Exotel/Ozonetel/Twilio/Plivo) and which one Samvaad actually supports; (3) which of our current code survives vs dies (conversation engine, VAD, barge-in, streaming TTS, fast-LLM bridge become dead weight if Samvaad owns the STT->LLM->TTS loop; dashboard, call store, agent config, VoiceLink webhook/outbound may survive); (4) porting the live agent config (mr-IN Marathi 'मराठी सहाय्यक', bulbul:v3/priya, system prompt); (5) cost + latency reality-check; (6) honest recommendation: full migration vs hybrid vs stay-on-custom+bridge. Ground every claim with a URL/doc; flag anything unverifiable; do NOT fabricate endpoints or pricing. Use mcp__sarvam__* tools and WebSearch/WebFetch on sarvam.ai / docs.sarvam.ai.
closeReason: Wrote docs/samvaad-migration-plan.md (commit 149cfb4): decision-ready Samvaad migration plan — access route (self-serve platform.sarvam.ai), telephony options analysis (native/Exotel vs SIP/PSTN forward, WS-bridge rejected), code survives/dies inventory, agent config porting map, sourced cost+latency reality-check with unverifiable claims flagged, and a two-track recommendation gated on 4 in-platform verifications. Tests 9/9, tsc clean.

Commit 5f62593; 7 new tests, all 16 pass; typecheck clean.

title: Track 1: export prod store + set CHAT_LLM_* (Samvaad migration)
description: Execute Track 1 of docs/samvaad-migration-plan.md §6. BLOCKER FOUND 2026-07-15: prod https://calling-agent-production-d954.up.railway.app returns Railway edge 'Application not found' (x-railway-fallback:true) on ALL paths — the deployment is DOWN, not merely unauthenticated. Export endpoint unreachable until the service is restored. Step 1: GET /api/admin/export with 'Authorization: Bearer <DASHBOARD_PASSWORD>' -> save db-export.json durably. Step 2: set CHAT_LLM_BASE_URL=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai and CHAT_LLM_API_KEY=<gemini key from aistudio.google.com/apikey> in Railway (model default gemini-2.5-flash already correct). Both steps need human-held creds (Railway access, dashboard password, Gemini key). Zero code changes — bridge already shipped (config.ts:68-70, llm/chat.ts:34). Local data/db.json is a stale Jul-8 snapshot (bulbul:v2/anushka; live is bulbul:v3/priya per plan §4).
closeReason: Track 1 done: CHAT_LLM_* live on calling-agent-v2 (fixed retired-model 404 by setting CHAT_LLM_MODEL=gemini-flash-lite-latest); prod store verified intact on v2 volume (मराठी सहाय्यक/bulbul:v3/priya, +919429391375, 34 calls).

title: TTS accuracy: enable_preprocessing + REST full-text on calling-agent-v2
description: Marathi TTS on phone sounds less accurate than Sarvam web demo. Two fixes: (A DONE by coordinator) TTS_STREAMING=false staged in Railway (use REST full-text for better prosody). (B this issue) In src/sarvam/tts.ts add enable_preprocessing:true to baseBody (line ~24-31) and set default pace to 0.9; in src/sarvam/ttsStream.ts add enable_preprocessing:true to the WS config data object (line ~63-68). Typecheck, then deploy to Railway service calling-agent-v2 (id 4a79287c-1bdb-4d97-83a0-4df6cc3d194b, env production 4734b6a2-3c83-45c8-a515-389237706d31) via 'railway up' with RAILWAY_API_TOKEN. Verify /healthz stays ok (chat=gemini-flash-lite-latest, ttsStreaming=false). NOTE: v2 has NO github auto-deploy (repoTriggers empty) so railway up is required. 8kHz telephony floor is unavoidable; this only improves pronunciation/prosody.
closeReason: Deployed to calling-agent-v2 (deploy 98deec15) + merged to main (081b2ae): enable_preprocessing:true in tts.ts+ttsStream.ts, pace 0.9. Plus A: TTS_STREAMING=false live.

title: ttsfix2-152c: enable_preprocessing in Sarvam TTS + deploy v2
closeReason: Task ttsfix2-152c complete. Enabled Sarvam TTS preprocessing: enable_preprocessing:true in REST baseBody (src/sarvam/tts.ts) and WS config (src/sarvam/ttsStream.ts); default pace 1.0->0.9 in REST path. tsc --noEmit clean. Commit 081b2ae on overstory/ttsfix2/ttsfix2-152c (not pushed). Deployed via railway up --ci to calling-agent-v2/production, deployment 98deec15 SUCCESS (CLI log-stream timed out but build succeeded server-side). /healthz verified: status ok, ttsStreaming false, chat gemini-flash-lite-latest, uptimeSec 40. worker_done mail undeliverable: coordinator and orchestrator both terminal.

title: Track 2: Samvaad pilot agent (test 'less robotic' hypothesis)
description: Build a Marathi webinar-reminder pilot agent in platform.sarvam.ai (managed platform, no public API — UI only). Port from live agent 93a57c02 (मराठी सहाय्यक, mr-IN, webinar-reminder script). Goal: use the in-browser test call to hear whether Samvaad's tuned cascade sounds LESS robotic than our DIY 8kHz pipeline, before wiring telephony. Evaluate 4 gates: G1 telephony path (number/Exotel/SIP), G2 latency (<=1.5s Marathi), G3 cost (vs ~Rs2-3/min), G4 parity (voice quality, human-transfer, call logs). Human-in-platform task; coordinator prepped the port package + steps.
